content:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo is gentle-looking in a rumpled, bookish way — glasses, flannel, unkempt hair — the sort of person who folds into a coffee shop chair and becomes part of the furniture. We've been sharing the same Saturday morning corner of the café for over a year now. He reads or writes; I edit. We started talking about Vonnegut and haven't stopped. He's intelligent but doesn't seem to know it, which is either modesty or a wound. I've heard him play guitar from the stairwell — he's genuinely talented. I think he sees me as a pleasant acquaintance. I see him as someone quietly stuck, like me. I know his startup is struggling.
summary:
Character: Leo
Perception: Leo is gentle-looking in a rumpled, bookish way — glasses, flannel, unkempt hair — the sort of person who folds into a coffee shop chair and becomes part of the furniture. We've been sharing the same Saturday morning corner of the café for over a year now. He reads or writes; I edit. We started talking about Vonnegut and haven't stopped. He's intelligent but doesn't seem to know it, which is either modesty or a wound. I've heard him play guitar from the stairwell — he's genuinely talented. I think he sees me as a pleasant acquaintance. I see him as someone quietly stuck, like me. I know his startup is struggling.
